info.title: PayCan API Documentation
info.description: Comprehensive API documentation for PayCan payment processing platform. Supports Stripe and PayPal payments, subscription management, and order processing.

get.description: Retrieve paginated and filterable list of all products (admin only)
Retrieve a specific product by ID (admin only)
Get all prices for a specific product

post.description: Create a new price for a product

get.description: Retrieve paginated and filterable list of all transactions (admin only)
Retrieve a specific transaction by ID (admin only)

post.description: Authenticate user and generate access token
Register a new user account and generate access token
Revoke current access token and logout user
Generate a new access token and revoke the current one
Generate an API token for authenticated user (alternative to login for existing sessions)

get.description: Retrieve a paginated and filterable list of all orders (admin only)
Retrieve a specific order by ID (admin only)

post.description: Create a checkout session for one-time payment
Create a new order for one-time payment or subscription

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of active products with optional filtering, sorting, and includes

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort products by field. Use '-' prefix for descending order

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of items per page (default: 10, max: 100)

get.description: Retrieve a specific active product by ID with optional includes

Retrieve paginated and filterable list of current user's orders with optional related data. Requires user JWT token authentication. Example: apiClient.setUserToken(jwt); await apiClient.orders.list();
Retrieve a specific order by ID for the current user
Get download links for digital products in an order
Get license keys for an order

Retrieve paginated and filterable list of active products with their active prices. Requires user JWT token authentication (generated via Sanctum). Example usage in Payment Portal: apiClient.setUserToken(jwt); await apiClient.products.list();
Retrieve a specific active product by ID with its active prices

404.description: Product not found or not active

get.description: Retrieve paginated and filterable list of current user's transactions
Retrieve a specific transaction by ID for the current user

post.summary: Generate JWT token for external user
post.description: Creates or updates a user and returns a JWT token. Requires API key authentication. Uses user_id as the unique identifier. For NEW users: name and email are required. For EXISTING users: updates are optional.

user_id.description: User ID - will be used as the user's primary key

user.description: User data - REQUIRED for new users (must include name and email), OPTIONAL for existing users (updates provided fields)

name.description: User's name (required for new users, optional for updates)

email.description: User's email (required for new users, optional for updates)

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

error.example: The requested resource was not found

message.example: The requested resource was not found

from.description: First item number on current page

to.description: Last item number on current page

sanctum.description: USER TOKEN AUTHENTICATION (JWT) - For end-user operations in frontend applications (payment portals, customer dashboards). Generate using Laravel Sanctum: $user->createToken('token-name')->plainTextToken. Use for: User-specific operations (orders, subscriptions, profile). Format: Bearer {jwt_token}. NEVER use API Key for frontend authentication.

apiKey.description: API KEY AUTHENTICATION (Admin/Backend Only) - For server-to-server integrations & admin operations. Get from Admin Panel > Settings > API Secret Key. Use for: /api/admin/* routes (Product management, Transaction management). Format: Bearer pk_xxxxx. NEVER expose API Key in frontend code.

apiKeyHeader.description: API Secret Key in X-API-Key header. Alternative to Authorization Bearer token.
apiKeyHeader.name: X-API-Key

apiKeyQuery.description: API Secret Key in query parameter. Format: ?api_key=pk_xxxxx... (not recommended for production)
apiKeyQuery.name: api_key
